# UC-11.3.62 · Proofpoint TAP Phishing Classification Volume by Sender Domain

## Description

Aggregating Proofpoint TAP events classified as Phish by sending domain highlights persistent phishing infrastructure, compromised senders, and bulk campaigns targeting the tenant.

## Value

Turns TAP’s condemnation metadata into domain-level situational awareness for threat intel feeds, firewall blocks, and executive reporting without exporting CSVs from Proofpoint.

## Implementation

Ensure `classification` is extracted; multi-threat messages may list multiple classes—use `mvexpand` on `threatsInfoMap` if counts look low. Normalize `sender` formats (envelope vs header). Join top domains to WHOIS or intel lookups. Schedule daily with adaptive thresholds for seasonal spikes.

## SPL

```spl
index=email sourcetype="proofpoint_tap_siem" earliest=-7d
| eval cls=coalesce(classification, Classification, "")
| where cls="Phish"
| eval from_addr=coalesce(sender, Sender, fromAddress, FromAddress, "")
| eval dom=replace(from_addr, "^[^@]+@", "")
| stats count by dom
| sort -count
```

## Visualization

Bar chart (top domains), pie (internal vs external domains), timechart of phish events.

## References

- [Proofpoint TAP Modular Input for Splunk](https://splunkbase.splunk.com/app/3681)
- [Proofpoint TAP SIEM API](https://help.proofpoint.com/Threat_Insight_Dashboard/API_Documentation/SIEM_API)
